_notes.purpose: Corridor-level layer for India → United States export-import scenarios with no commodity override. Carries the Fed-path inputs (importer = US side) and the sovereign spread inputs (exporter = IN side) that are commodity-independent. Tariff and working-capital inputs are commodity-specific and remain in the commodity-leaf layer (e.g. india-us-textiles.json).
_notes.merge_position: Middle. Loaded by profile_resolver after export-import/_base.json and before any commodity leaf. Inherits the 4-component skeleton, stress_timing, tenor_grid, loan_spread_default, mode, and dispatch from _base; merges these inputs on top.
_notes.source_of_values: V1 SWAPS-1LOAN-WHAT-IF-3-INP-FINAL.json corridor table 'India-United States': sovereign 50bps base / 70bps peak; FED_PATH initial=0.0450, peak=0.0550, final=0.0475.

source.note: V1 inline FED_PATH (initial 4.50%, peak 5.50%, final 4.75%). corridor-references file scaffolded in Iteration 2+; until populated, values live here.

source.citation: S&P BBB (Aug 2025), 50bps base / 70bps peak per V1 CORRIDOR_PARAMETERS for India-United States.
